name: Microsoft 365
description: Access Outlook mail, calendar, OneDrive files, Excel, contacts, tasks, and other Microsoft 365 services through AI chat via the Microsoft Graph API.

oauth_config.scopes: User.Read Mail.ReadWrite Calendars.ReadWrite Files.ReadWrite.All Contacts.ReadWrite Tasks.ReadWrite Notes.ReadWrite.All offline_access

user_auth_instructions:
1. Click 'Connect' to link your Microsoft 365 account.
2. Sign in with your work or school Microsoft account.
3. Review and approve the requested permissions.
4. You'll be redirected back automatically.

Note: Your organization admin must first register an Azure AD application.
setup_notes:
Requires an Azure AD (Entra ID) app registration with delegated Microsoft Graph permissions.

Admin setup:
1. Go to https://portal.azure.com → Azure Active Directory → App registrations → New registration
2. Set redirect URI: {your_app_url}/api/v1/mcp-servers/{server_id}/oauth/callback
3. Under 'Certificates & secrets', create a new client secret
4. Under 'API permissions', add Microsoft Graph delegated permissions:
   - User.Read, Mail.ReadWrite, Calendars.ReadWrite
   - Files.ReadWrite.All, Contacts.ReadWrite
   - Tasks.ReadWrite, Notes.ReadWrite.All
5. Grant admin consent for the permissions
6. Enter the Application (client) ID, Client Secret, and Tenant ID in the server configuration

Set MS365_MCP_TENANT_ID to your Azure AD tenant ID, or 'common' for multi-tenant apps.

Note: Microsoft also offers official Work IQ MCP servers
(Mail, Calendar, OneDrive, Teams, SharePoint, Word)
but these require a Microsoft 365 Copilot license.
See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-agent-365/tooling-servers-overview
